reasoning: Intuitive Machines has a genuinely differentiated catalyst with 'Big Contract, Big Backlog' news suggesting material revenue visibility improvement. The 6.2% single-day drop amid broader space sector volatility (SpaceX IPO noise) creates a potential dislocation. However, the company remains pre-profitability with execution risk, warranting a watchlist note rather than full initiation until we see Q1 results and backlog conversion details.
title: Intuitive Machines: NASA's Lunar Bet Gets Real—But So Does Execution Risk
angle: The NASA CLPS contract backlog is building genuine revenue visibility for 2026-27, but the market is conflating LUNR with speculative space plays. The edge is in parsing which backlog is firm-fixed-price vs. cost-plus, and modeling realistic mission cadence. A watchlist note frames the thesis while we await clearer unit economics.
